title: Adversarial Test-Likeness Reweighting
summary: The train/test overview already shows distribution shift risk across years, races, and driver identities.
rationale: The train/test overview already shows distribution shift risk across years, races, and driver identities. Importance weighting is a standard response to covariate shift, and this competition’s unseen or differently mixed race contexts make that more plausible than another round of static feature additions.
implementation_hint: Train a lightweight train-vs-test discriminator on features only, convert its scores into bounded sample weights, and refit the main classifier with those weights. Compare against unweighted training under strict Race-Year grouped CV, and optionally blend weighted and unweighted OOF models.
expected_effect: Can improve leaderboard robustness if the public test set emphasizes a different mixture of seasons, drivers, or race regimes than local CV.
risk: Noisy weights can raise variance or hurt if the shift detector mostly learns leakage-prone identifiers rather than meaningful covariate drift.
